danger_signals.meaning: Instant death hazard; touching causes life loss
Contact hazard; touching causes life loss

danger_signals.meaning: Spatial danger zones indicating where enemies will be

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Tater Troopers (helmet-wearing potato men); various squash types with different movement patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outposts and cities of Tuberia; Mount Tuberest castle (final stronghold); vegetable-themed architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Commander Keen helmet; represents child protagonist and resistance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bed/home setting (Earth); Dream Machine spatial domain; enslaved children visual indicators

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pajama-clad Keen in vegetable kingdom environment; surreal landscape with vegetable enemies; giant helmet-wearing potato men
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Keen forced into slavery by Boobus Tuber; other children appear in chains; enemies patrol and attack

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential level design moving through outposts and cities toward castle on mountain summit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Keen must collect Boobus Bombs to prepare for final boss; enemies increase in complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enslaved children present in environment; dream landscape persists
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Keen must defeat Boobus Tuber to deactivate machine and free captives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from vegetable kingdom to bedroom setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Keen wakes in bed at home; mother announces 'I Hate Broccoli' day (thematic closure)

environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from outposts → cities of Tuberia → Mount Tuberest castle visualizes escalating challenge and power structure of vegetable kingdom
Different vegetable enemy types (potatoes, squash) with distinct silhouettes and patrol patterns establish territorial domains and faction diversity
Enslaved children shown in chains within levels communicate the oppressive Dream Machine regime
Boobus Tuber positioned at Mount Tuberest peak signals him as kingdom's apex authority; final confrontation location reinforces political hierarchy
All enemies are anthropomorphic vegetables; architecture and environment reinforce thematic coherence of dream logic and parental authority over eating vegetables

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Keen's weapon from prior games is non-functional
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Signals powerlessness in dream state; flower power substitution indicates different rules govern this world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Predictable movement patterns; attack on contact; vulnerable to flower power transformation
Distinct movement patterns from potatoes; contact causes life loss; transformable via flower power
Become passive, intangible flowers for limited duration; cease threat
Present in chains; run toward Keen requesting his intervention; visual indicators of oppression
Controls Dream Machine; position at Mount Tuberest peak; requires defeat via collected bombs
Assumes helmet for combat mode; collects resources; progresses toward castle; transforms enemies non-lethally

notes: Keen Dreams uses dream-logic world-state to communicate an inverted power dynamic: vegetables as enemies rather than food subjects, reflecting parental authority through vegetable-consumption conflict. The game communicates faction identity primarily through enemy type morphology (potatoes vs. squash) rather than color coding. World state transitions are marked by spatial progression (outposts → cities → castle) and visual presence of enslaved children as oppression markers. The substitution of flower power for raygun signals rule-set changes in dream domain. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on level design progression and thematic vegetable cohesion rather than explicit narrative markers.
